q: The Keeling Curve shows annual oscillations in atmospheric CO2 superimposed on a rising trend. What explains the oscillations?
options: Seasonal changes in volcanic activity cause CO2 to rise in summer and fall in winter

Seasonal variation in photosynthesis and respiration in the northern hemisphere: CO2 falls in northern spring/summer (photosynthesis dominates) and rises in autumn/winter (respiration and decay dominate)
Ocean currents shift annually, absorbing more CO2 in winter and releasing it in summer
Human fossil fuel combustion peaks in summer due to industrial cooling demands and decreases in winter
explanation: The seasonal oscillation reflects northern hemisphere growing seasons; widespread plant growth in spring/summer draws down CO2 via photosynthesis, while leaf fall and decomposition in autumn/winter releases it back.
